items.question: Who benefits from wrapping cleaning and transformation in a sklearn Pipeline?
items.answer: Data scientists and ML engineers building tabular models who need reproducible preprocessing across cross-validation, hyperparameter search, batch retraining, and production scoring—especially when numeric and categorical columns require different transform chains.

items.question: What is a cleaning and transformation pipeline in the pandas/sklearn sense?
items.answer: An ordered chain of hygiene steps (dedupe, dtype fix, string normalize) and learnable transformers (imputer, encoder, scaler) bundled in Pipeline and ColumnTransformer, fit on training data and replayed unchanged at inference.

items.question: When should you split data relative to pipeline fitting?
items.answer: Before fitting any transformer that learns statistics—split (or define CV folds) first, then call pipeline.fit on the training partition only; never fit imputers or scalers on the full dataset prior to splitting.

items.question: Where do imputation and scaling theory live versus pipeline orchestration?
items.answer: Imputation mechanisms and strategy choice are canonical in missing values; StandardScaler and MinMaxScaler formulas and when-to-scale rules are in scaling and normalization; this topic wires those transformers in the correct order inside Pipeline without re-deriving the theory.

items.question: Why must imputation precede scaling in a numeric branch?
items.answer: Scalers compute mean and variance on finite values; NaN breaks or distorts those statistics. Imputing first yields a complete training matrix so the scaler learns well-defined parameters applied consistently to validation and production rows.

items.question: How does ColumnTransformer differ from a single linear Pipeline?
items.answer: ColumnTransformer runs separate mini-pipelines on specified column subsets (e.g. numeric vs categorical), fits each branch on its columns, and concatenates outputs—whereas a simple Pipeline applies the same step sequence to the entire input matrix.

items.question: How does a fitted Pipeline compare to saving only the final estimator?
items.answer: A fitted Pipeline serializes imputer medians, encoder categories, scaler statistics, and model weights together; saving only the estimator forces serving code to reimplement or omit transforms, causing train/serve skew and wrong predictions on raw DataFrames.

items.question: How does pandas in-notebook cleaning compare to FunctionTransformer inside Pipeline?
items.answer: Notebook cells run once during exploration and are easy to skip in production APIs; FunctionTransformer (or custom transformers) embed the same hygiene in the artifact so fit/transform and joblib load paths apply identical cleaning automatically.

items.question: How does manual preprocess-then-CV compare to Pipeline with cross_val_score?
items.answer: Manual scaling or imputation on all of X before CV leaks validation-fold statistics into preprocessing; Pipeline inside cross_val_score refits transforms on each fold's train slice only, matching honest evaluation and deployment discipline.
